title: Ansispire EDA Event (reactor input contract)
description: The minimal shape that reactor.py expects to read off events.jsonl. Outer wrapper fields (ts, remote, path, ua) added by audit-sink are tolerated and ignored. The 'payload' key carries the inner event used for rule matching. Two writers feed jsonl today: (a) audit-relay forwarding a Semaphore event via the sink (sink wraps with ts/path/etc; relay body is { source, event } so the resulting jsonl line has payload.source + payload.event), and (b) operators injecting test events directly (no sink wrapping, jsonl line is {payload:{event:{...}}}). Both paths converge on payload.event being the rule-matchable inner object.

ts.description: Wall-clock timestamp added by audit-sink. Absent on direct injection.

source.description: Set by audit-relay. Constant 'semaphore' for now.

event.description: Inner event object — this is what reactor matches rules against. Field set is open; fields below are the canonical ones rules.json may reference today. Add to this list when extending rule matching to a new field.

status.description: Lifecycle status, e.g. waiting / running / success / error.
